title: carum atlanticum
intro: Carum atlanticum, also known as Sea Carrot or Wild Carrot, is a perennial plant native to the Mediterranean region and parts of Europe, characterized by its white or pinkish flowers and edible roots. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of carvone and limonene compounds. The health benefits of Carum atlanticum include relief from digestive issues, reduced risk of cancer, and improved immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of ailments, including respiratory issues and skin conditions.
